steps.title: Use f(6) = 42 to find k
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 6$ into the function definition:
$$f(6) = 5(6) + k(6-2) = 42$$
$$30 + 4k = 42$$
$$4k = 12$$
$$k = 3$$

steps.title: Evaluate f(11) with k = 3
steps.content:
Now that $k = 3$, simplify the original function:
$$f(x) = 5x + 3(x-2) = 5x + 3x - 6 = 8x - 6$$
Evaluate at $x = 11$:
$$f(11) = 8(11) - 6 = 88 - 6 = 82$$
